source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT MANAGER - LIMBIC ACCESS

Limbic builds AI-powered mental healthcare. Our front-door Access product talks to thousands of patients a month across the NHS and major US health systems. Access is past 0→1 in the UK and US: the product exists, customers are live, the model is proven. We're hiring a Product Manager to take it 1→10 in the US, against a growing pipeline of enterprise customers, owning the team's day-to-day product cadence. The job is to ship and ship well.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

You'll own the week-to-week rhythm that turns engineering into shipped product:

 - Run the team's ceremonies: effective, alignment-creating, always followed up on.

 - Own roadmap prioritisation. Understand the projects in play, weigh their relative impact, and sequence them. Ground the sequencing in customer signal, and user feedback, usage, and behaviour.

 - Triage the inbound: bugs, debt, CS asks, scope additions. Separate real issues from noise, route to the right owner, decide what gets iterated when.

 - Scope and define the work. Write project overviews the team can act on, ensuring the next-highest-value project is always lined up.

 - Own the incident loop. Triage, root cause, fix, follow-up. Engineers get space to fix; you hold the loop.

 - Keep the product well instrumented. Mixpanel today and whatever's next. Ensure the team can answer its own questions from the data, and that you can read user behavior out of it.

 - Protect engineering focus. Hold the line on scope creep from any sources. Check in 1:1 mid-cycle with anyone who's blocked or drifting.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You've shipped product before. Maybe one or two roles in. You want real product management. The kind where you own outcomes, not Jira tickets.

 - You ship features and projects that move metrics and create outcomes. Not work that's about looking good.

 - Comfortable with engineers and engineering concepts. You build strong mental models for how products work and apply them in your day-to-day.

 - You write clearly and quickly. You think clearly from first principles.

 - You think critically with data. You can pull insights yourself, interrogate how they were generated, and reason about what they actually mean. Not 'I made a decision with data once.'

 - You never produce AI slop, but you do heavily rely on AI in your day-to-day. You go beyond using AI to write PRDs.

 - You are 'client safe' - you can represent Limbic to customers professionally.

 - You can say no to someone and make them feel it's in their best interest.

 - You build products. You know the difference between a feature that creates repeatable value and a one-off that introduces drag and tech debt.

 - Healthcare experience is a bonus, but not a requirement. EHR integration and working knowledge of health-data standards (HL7, FHIR) especially.

You won't fit if you need polished process, a heavy spec phase, or a team that loves meetings. We're ahead of our process.




WHY THIS ROLE

Two things to know.

One. It's a delivery role for now, with a clear set of projects to execute on (but prioritize and sequence effectively). You'll grow into more strategic scope over the next 4-6 months. The immediate brief is to make the engineering team's product cadence quieter and more predictable.

Two. The team is small and load is high. The hire exists because we need to free senior product time for discovery and strategy on a separate product. You might feel the pressure of that. In return: real customers (millions of lives) and the opportunity to iterate the product in a company-defining direction.




PRACTICAL

 - Level: APM / Mid Product Manager

 - Location: East Coast USA

source_jobs.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Help us build the future of mental health by developing our new AI Therapist for end-to-end care in the US.

About Limbic

Limbic’s vision is to ensure the highest quality therapy is accessible to everyone, everywhere.

Limbic is already used in over 50% of Talking Therapies in the NHS, which makes us the largest-ever deployment of generative AI for direct patient care. Using Limbic Access we augment clinical care and reduce barriers to accessing therapy at scale - deployed with safety as the top priority and the only AI mental health chatbot with UKCA Class IIa certification.

Limbic is live in the US, interacting with thousands of patients there every week. Furthermore, we are launching an AI therapist doing end-to-end care in the US, and we are looking for a mission-driven engineer to help us build this critical new product.

The Role

As a Senior ML/AI Engineer, you have demonstrated experience in AI product development and will take ownership of translating AI research into products that tangibly improve users' lives. This role is specifically dedicated to building our end-to-end care AI therapist for the US market. Initially, your projects will focus on this immediate launch, but you will eventually be tasked with bringing these successes cross-product to the wider organization.

What You’ll Do

 - Productionise ML models to optimise product KPIs, including algorithmic patient selection to maximise recovery rates.

 - Build and maintain agentic frameworks whils ensuring our AI features continuously comply with strict regulatory protocols.

 - Identify and model causal factors (such as AI skills) that determine our key performance indicators.

 - Productionise agentic frameworks for rapid product development, including building agents to automate product development workflows.

 - Productionise our product demo processes to streamline resources and prevent major issues during client demonstrations.

What You’ll Bring

 - You must meet one of the following educational and experience backgrounds:
   
   - A PhD in computational science or an AI-related field, plus 2 years of experience in an ML engineering role.
   
   - OR A BSc/MSc, plus 4 years of experience in an ML engineering role.

 - Expertise in productising LLM features and a strong background in Eval-driven-development.

 - Expert-level proficiency in Python and Typescript/Javascript.

 - Proven experience building an LLM agent harness.

 - Experience utilizing reinforcement learning.

 - Strong architectural skills with experience designing scalable, production-grade systems for machine learning applications.

 - A solid understanding of statistical principles specifically in the context of LLM evaluation and user activity analytics.

 - Proficiency with version control systems (preferably Git) and databases (SQL or NoSQL).

 - Hands-on experience with major cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerization technologies (Docker).

 - A genuine interest in applying your technical skills to solve complex challenges in the mental healthcare space.

 - The ability to work from our London (Spitalfields) office on Tuesdays and Thursdays, with the flexibility to work remotely or from the office on other days of the week.

 - Please note: Limbic cannot provide visa sponsorship.

Benefits & Wellbeing

 - Competitive salary and equity share options.

 - An amazing office in central London (Spitalfields) for our collaborative team days on Tuesdays and Thursdays, paired with a flexible/hybrid working model on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays.

 - 25 days PTO plus bank holidays.

 - Company pension scheme (UK).

 - Enhanced parental leave packages (UK).

 - Support with purchasing work-related books and materials.

 - Quarterly Life Days: Enjoy 4 paid days off per year (one each quarter) to use whenever you choose to relax, recharge, or take care of personal matters.

 - Mental Health Support: Access to dedicated mental health support services.

source_jobs.title: ABM & Marketing Executive
source_jobs.description:
Reporting to: UK General Manager
Location: London / UK hybrid or remote
Level: Junior / Early career



ABOUT US

We are a clinical AI company with a clear mission: using evidence-based clinical AI to make quality mental healthcare accessible to everyone, everywhere.

We believe technology can play a meaningful role in improving access to mental healthcare, reducing pressure on overstretched services, and supporting better outcomes for patients - but only when it is built responsibly, grounded in evidence, and designed around the realities of care delivery.

Our work in the UK is focused on supporting NHS Talking Therapies and wider mental health services. As a small, ambitious team, we are helping drive real change in how mental healthcare is delivered across the UK.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for an ABM & Marketing Executive to help grow our presence across the UK mental health market.

This is a junior but high-impact marketing role with a strong focus on account-based marketing, supporting targeted engagement with priority accounts across NHS Talking Therapies and wider mental health services. You will work closely with the UK General Manager to help shape and implement strategies for key accounts, identify the right organisations and stakeholders, and improve how we engage the market.

Alongside ABM activity, you will also support broader marketing initiatives across content, campaigns, webinars, events, CRM, and day-to-day execution. This is a hands-on role for someone who wants high autonomy, real responsibility, and the chance to learn fast inside a mission-driven AI company. You should be excited by the opportunity to test ideas, take ownership, and embrace the latest AI marketing tools to help us build a smarter, more modern marketing engine.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

· Support the planning and delivery of account-based marketing activity across priority UK accounts

· Research target organisations, services, and stakeholders to help build account plans, contact lists, and engagement strategies

· Help shape, set up, and implement targeted engagement strategies for priority accounts

· Coordinate tailored outreach and campaign activity across channels including email, LinkedIn, webinars, events, and follow-up communications

· Work closely with the UK General Manager and wider team to ensure account engagement is well organised, relevant, and timely

· Support the execution of broader UK marketing campaigns and initiatives

· Help create and adapt marketing assets including one-pagers, case studies, email copy, social content, event materials, and presentations

· Use the latest AI marketing tools to improve research, content creation, campaign execution, and productivity, while maintaining high standards of quality and accuracy

· Maintain CRM and campaign data, support audience segmentation, and help track engagement and performance

· Assist with webinars, roundtables, conferences, and other market-facing activity

· Contribute to market research, campaign reporting, and day-to-day marketing coordination

· Take ownership of projects and deliver them with a high degree of autonomy



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

· 1-3 years of experience in a marketing role, internship, graduate programme, or similar commercial environment

· A strong interest in B2B marketing, with enthusiasm for account-based marketing and targeted campaign execution

· Excitement about using the latest AI tools to work smarter, test ideas faster, and improve marketing effectiveness

· Excellent written communication skills and strong attention to detail

· Good organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities

· A proactive, hands-on approach and willingness to support across a range of marketing activities

· Comfort with autonomy, ownership, and a fast-moving startup environment

· Confidence working with different stakeholders in a collaborative, high-trust team

· Genuine interest in healthcare, mental health, and

source_jobs.title: Peer Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LIMBIC

Limbic is the leading clinical AI company for mental healthcare and the largest deployment of patient-facing mental health AI in the world. Our technology has supported over half a million patients across the UK and US, helping health systems deliver higher-quality, more accessible, and more efficient care.

We regulated the first AI diagnostic model for adult mental health as a Class IIa medical device. We’ve published in top-tier journals, won major awards, and advise governments and regulators globally on AI safety, medical devices, and the future of clinical agentic systems.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for an experienced peer support specialist to join our team as an independent contractor (1099). You will use your lived experience to motivate patients and build trust within an AI-led therapy program. This role requires being a steady, human presence for others, especially when navigating technological challenges. This fully virtual position offers the flexibility to design a schedule that works for you.

We are building a team to reach patients who have been underserved by traditional systems, including older adults, rural residents, and people from marginalized backgrounds whose identities deserve genuine understanding.

 - Position Type: 1099 Independent Contractor

 - Location: Fully remote / Telehealth

 - Schedule: Flexible within clinic hours (Monday-Friday 9:00-5:00pm EST)

 - Compensation: Fee-for-service
   
   - Each session, inclusive of documentation and required administration, is paid at $10 (estimated session length 20-30 minutes).
   
   - Training on Limbic systems, essential training to ensure patient safety, and coordination meetings are paid at $20/hour, prorated.
   
   - Paid no-show fee, reflecting the documentation, patient outreach, and scheduling work required regardless of session attendance.


CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Individual Support: Deliver peer sessions that normalize challenges, instill hope, and reinforce therapeutic progress.

 - Patient-Centered Motivation: Meet individuals where they are, helping those who feel hesitant find their own internal reasons for engagement.

 - Group Sessions: Lead virtual groups focused on wellbeing tools and psycho-education.

 - Escalation: Follow crisis protocols to assess safety and initiate clinical handoffs to psychotherapists when necessary.

Note: Maintaining boundaries and scope of practice is vital. You will never be asked to perform clinical duties or therapy that requires a professional license.




WHO WE NEED

 - Credentialing: A current and unrestricted peer support certification (e.g., CPS-MH or state equivalent). Certified Older Adult Peer Specialist (COAPS) or Digital Certified Older Adult Peer Support Specialists (D-COAPS) a plus.

 - Lived Experience: Personal history of anxiety and/or depression that you can leverage to instill hope. Health-tech experience is a plus.

 - Inclusivity: An authentic commitment to meeting every patient with curiosity and respect, ensuring those from marginalized backgrounds feel genuinely seen.

 - Older Adults: A strong interest in supporting adults 65+ with patience and awareness of their unique medical and generational needs.

 - Facilitation: Experience leading peer or educational groups is a plus.

 - Tech Aptitude: Comfort with digital platforms and a methodical approach to troubleshooting hiccups with patience.

 - Candidates must have regular access to a private computer/laptop and high-speed internet access while conducting sessions

 - Candidates must have a private space to make calls to patients where they will not be overheard.

 - Experience in mental health coaching a plus.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Paid training, meetings, and no show fees.

 - Flexible, remote schedule within clinic hours (Monday-Friday 9:00-5:00pm EST)

 - The chance to collaborate with experts and shape the future of AI in mental healthcare.

 - A supportive clinical environment with direct access to supe

source_jobs.title: AI/ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Help us build the future of mental health by developing our new AI Therapist for end-to-end care in the US.

About Limbic

Limbic’s vision is to ensure the highest quality therapy is accessible to everyone, everywhere.

Limbic is already used in over 50% of Talking Therapies in the NHS, which makes us the largest-ever deployment of generative AI for direct patient care. Using Limbic Access we augment clinical care and reduce barriers to accessing therapy at scale - deployed with safety as the top priority and the only AI mental health chatbot with UKCA Class IIa certification.

Limbic is live in the US, interacting with thousands of patients there every week. Furthermore, we are launching an AI therapist doing end-to-end care in the US, and we are looking for a mission-driven engineer to help us build this critical new product.

The Role

As an AI/ML Engineer, you will sit at the intersection of computational psychiatry, clinical science, and software engineering. You will drive the development of AI interventions that engage users, predict recovery milestones, and orchestrate evidence-based therapeutic sessions. This role is specifically focused on building and refining our end-to-end care AI therapist for the US market.

What You’ll Do

 - Drive effective session orchestration and lead the implementation of our Evidence-Based Intervention Library.

 - Develop AI features tailored for product retention and engagement.

 - Implement features involving expectation setting, gamification, and reinforcement learning for the personalization of care.

 - Optimize our recovery data collection processes, focusing on the methods, timing, and predictive modelling of PHQ-9s.

 - Provide occasional analysis and modelling support for additional clinical research projects.

What You’ll Bring

 - You must meet one of the following educational and experience backgrounds:
   
   - A PhD in Computational Psychiatry or a related field, coupled with demonstrated industry experience in Data Science, Product, or ML engineering.
   
   - OR A BSc/MSc in a Computational Psychiatry related field (research focus) plus 2 years of experience in ML engineering or Data Science within an Engineering/Product Development environment.

 - Proficiency in either Python or Typescript/Javascript.

 - An expert approach to hypothesis-driven work, with a track record of balancing product development with scientific rigour.

 - Experience in Eval-driven-development.

 - Experience using Machine Learning in a production environment or via high-impact scientific publications (e.g., predictive modelling, reinforcement learning).

 - Experience modelling text/language data, including building LLM classifiers and using vector-based methods.

 - Experience modelling longitudinal data and working with databases (SQL or NoSQL).

 - A strong understanding of evidence-based mental health care (such as Cognitive Behavioural Therapy) and a deep interest in the causal mechanisms that drive therapeutic benefit.

 - Proficiency with version control systems, preferably Git.

 - Ideal but not required: Experience building an LLM agent harness.

 - The ability to work from our London (Spitalfields) office on Tuesdays and Thursdays, with the flexibility to work remotely or from the office on other days of the week.

 - Please note: Limbic cannot provide visa sponsorship.

Benefits & Wellbeing

 - Competitive salary and equity share options.

 - An amazing office in central London (Spitalfields) for our collaborative team days on Tuesdays and Thursdays, paired with a flexible/hybrid working model on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays.

 - 25 days PTO plus bank holidays.

 - Company pension scheme (UK).

 - Enhanced parental leave packages (UK).

 - Support with purchasing work-related books and materials.

 - Quarterly Life Days: Enjoy 4 paid days off per year (one each quarter) to use whenever you choose to relax, recharge, or take care of personal matters.

 - Mental Health Support: Acc

source_jobs.title: Clinical Contractor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LIMBIC

Limbic is the company behind the world's largest deployment of patient-facing mental health AI, having supported over 500,000 patients to date. We built the first AI diagnostic for adult mental health to be regulated as a Class IIa medical device, our work is peer-reviewed and published in leading journals, and we advise governments globally.




THE ROLE

We're hiring a Clinical Contractor for a focused, four-week sprint to accelerate the development of Limbic Care, our patient-facing CBT app. You'll be an experienced psychologist or psychotherapist, specialising in CBT, who can test, iterate on, and write the prompts behind the generative AI that delivers care to our patients.

This is a hands-on, fast-moving contract. Onboarding time is minimal - we need someone who can begin prompt writing and iterating on existing prompts almost immediately. Full support will be available to you throughout the working week, but you should be comfortable hitting the ground running from day one.




AT A GLANCE

Contract length: 4 weeks

Time commitment: 20 hours per week

Pay: £50/hour (UK) or $68/hour (US)

Location: Remote - must be resident in the UK or the US

Start date: As soon as possible (within the next week)

Hours: Flexible, including outside typical working hours - but with at least 4 hours per week overlapping the UK working day to attend meetings if required.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Test and iterate on our current CBT interventions, as delivered by a generative AI chatbot - pressure-testing clinical content for quality, efficacy and safety.

 - Create new clinical content from scratch, including writing prompts that direct generative AI agents to deliver CBT.

 - Run clinical testing across various AI models to assure clinical efficacy and patient safety.
   
   


ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS

 - A clinician — psychologist or psychotherapist — with a qualification and extensive experience in delivering CBT.

 - Registered/licensed with an appropriate regulatory body. For UK applicants, this must be the BABCP. US applicants must hold equivalent professional licensure.

 - Experience working with or for an AI company, or extensive experience prompt engineering and testing clinical LLM conversations.

 - Not currently working with or for another company that creates AI products for use in mental or behavioural healthcare (conflict of interest).

 - Resident in the UK or the US.

 - Able to start as soon as possible (within the next week) and able to begin prompt engineering with minimal onboarding.

 - Fully available for the engagement: you must not have any periods of leave within the next month that would prevent you from completing 20 hours per week of work for Limbic.

 - You do not need to be able to code or be proficient in any form of software engineering. We provide the UI which allows you to utilise your clinical and prompt engineering/writing skills.
   
   


WAYS OF WORKING

You can complete your hours flexibly, including outside of typical working hours, provided at least 4 hours each week overlap with the UK working day so you can attend meetings. Onboarding will be light by design, but clinical and technical support will be available to you at all times within the working week.




WHY THIS HIRE MATTERS

The work you do over these four weeks will directly shape how Limbic Care supports patients - improving the clinical quality and safety of an AI that is already among the most widely used in mental healthcare.



Limbic is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of background.



If you are interested in this position, please apply asap as we may close early if we receive a high quality of applicants.

source_jobs.title: Supervising Psychotherapist (CA)
source_jobs.description:
SUPERVISING PSYCHOTHERAPIST (CA)




ABOUT LIMBIC

Limbic is the leading clinical AI company for mental healthcare and the largest deployment of patient-facing mental health AI in the world. Our technology has supported over half a million patients across the UK and US, helping health systems deliver higher-quality, more accessible, and more efficient care.

We regulated the first AI diagnostic model for adult mental health as a Class IIa medical device. We’ve published in top-tier journals, won major awards, and advise governments and regulators globally on AI safety, medical devices, and the future of clinical agentic systems.

We now want to focus on providing direct-to-patient best in class AI-led care, and are recruiting to staff our exciting and progressive care clinic.




BRIEF JOB DESCRIPTION:

We are seeking an experienced Psychotherapist or Psychologist to ensure clinical excellence within our Limbic Care Clinic. This role spans a multitude of exciting and varied tasks: you will supervise our team of Peer Support Specialists, review and maintain the clinical accuracy and safety of our AI therapy services, and act as the primary bridge for clinical escalations, moving cases from our digital platform to human-led intervention and reporting directly to the Head of Clinical Operations.

This role is presented part-time, but covering a full working week (eg. 2.5 hours per day Mon-Fri). We are also open to job-sharing opportunities.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Clinical Oversight: Supervise a team of Peer Support Specialists, providing performance monitoring, mentorship, and weekly supervision.

 - Escalation Management: Act as the frontline clinician for cases flagged by our AI systems or Peer Support team; perform direct outreach and risk assessment as needed.

 - System Quality Assurance: Conduct regular audits of AI conversation logs to ensure protocol adherence, clinical nuance, and patient safety.

 - Stakeholder Reporting: Monitor real-time clinical outcomes and escalate significant trends or safety concerns to the Medical Director.

 - Protocol Optimization: Use clinical insights from daily reviews to help refine and improve our automated patient pathways and safety guardrails.
   
   


WHO WE NEED

 - Clinical Credentials: Full, unrestricted psychotherapist (e.g., LCSW, LMHC, LPC) or Psychologist licensure. 3+ years post-licensure experience preferred but not essential.

 - California State Licensure: You MUST be licensed to practise within the state of CA. Additional licenses (including PSYPACT) is a plus.

 - Supervisory Experience: Proven track record in supervising or training clinicians or peer support staff.

 - Crisis Expertise: Advanced skills in risk assessment, crisis intervention, and safety planning.

 - Technical Aptitude: A strong interest in AI and the ability to evaluate machine-generated text through a clinical lens. You believe in the value AI can have in supporting patients within the US behavioural healthcare system. Previous experience working in AI/Mental Health Tech a plus.

 - Communication: Exceptional ability to translate complex clinical needs into actionable feedback for both human teams and technical stakeholders.
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Higher than market salary - $28,125 (based on the assumption of 12.5 hours per week) - equivalent to a $90,000 full time salary.

 - PTO in line with hours worked, plus federal holidays

 - 4 ‘Quarterly Life Days’ - one paid day off per quarter to use whenever you choose to relax, recharge, or take care of personal matters

 - Access to free mental health support

 - The opportunity to work alongside experts in their fields in a world-leading company

 - A chance to shape the future of mental health technology and AI safety protocols

 - An inclusive, collaborative, supportive working environment, where you will have direct access to clinical support and supervision at all times (within working hours)

 - Fully remote work with a culture that va

notes: Clinical AI company with a strong focus on product development and AI engineering.
